title: The Two Doors
prompt: You're in a room with two doors. One leads to freedom, one to danger. Two guards know which is which — one always lies, one always tells the truth. You can ask ONE question to ONE guard. What do you ask?
hint: Think about what question gives the same answer regardless of which guard you ask.

title: Reframe the Failure
prompt: Think of something you recently failed at or gave up on. Now rewrite it as a scientist would — not as failure, but as a data point. What did the experiment reveal?
hint: Scientists don't fail — they collect results. What result did you collect?

title: Alien Perspective
prompt: An alien visits Earth and watches humans use smartphones for one day. They write a report on what they observed. Write the first paragraph of that report.
hint: Strip away all assumptions. The alien has no context for what 'normal' means.

title: The Bridge Problem
prompt: 4 people need to cross a bridge at night with one torch. Max 2 cross at once. A=1min, B=2min, C=5min, D=10min. The pair moves at the slower person's pace. You have 17 minutes. How do they all cross?
hint: The key insight: who carries the torch back matters more than who crosses first.

title: 100 Uses
prompt: You have exactly 3 minutes. List as many uses as you can for a broken umbrella. No idea is too weird. Go.
hint: Think: material, shape, mechanism, metaphor, humor, destruction, art.

title: The Opposite Solution
prompt: Pick any problem you're currently dealing with. Now describe the WORST possible way to handle it. Then flip every element — what does the ideal solution look like now?
hint: Inversion reveals what you're avoiding. The opposite of bad isn't always obvious.

title: Daily Wordle
prompt: Guess the 5-letter word in 6 tries. Green = right letter right spot. Yellow = right letter wrong spot.
hint: Start with a word that uses common vowels: CRANE, SLATE, AUDIO.

title: Sudoku Sprint
prompt: Fill the 9x9 grid so every row, column, and 3x3 box contains digits 1-9.
hint: Start with rows/columns/boxes that have the most numbers already filled.

title: Chess Puzzle
prompt: White to move — find the checkmate in 2 moves.
hint: Look for a forcing move that limits all of the king's escape squares.
